entities.name: arthapravṛttitattvā
entities.cite: the realities that govern activity toward objects

entities.name: tattvāvabodhaḥ
entities.cite: there is no grasp of the reality of words

axioms.name: nibandhanam
axioms.cite: words are the nibandhana, the ground or fastening, of artha-pravrtti-tattva — the realities underlying our purposive activity toward objects

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the nature of words is not accessible without grammar

denials.name: asraya
denials.cite: the nature of words is not accessible without grammar

claims.name: nibandhanam
claims.cite: Words alone are the basis of the realities that govern activity toward objects

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: there is no grasp of the reality of words apart from grammar

rejected_readings.label: reversed_grounding
rejected_readings.rendering: The realities of activity provide the foundation for words; language is a consequence of objective structures.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the priority. The verse insists that words alone (sabda eva) are the nibandhanam of the realities underlying activity, not the reverse.

rejected_readings.label: grammar_optional
rejected_readings.rendering: Words can be understood independently of grammar; their essence is directly accessible without linguistic analysis.
rejected_readings.why: Directly contradicts the second premise. The commentary explicitly states there is no grasp (nasti) of word-essence apart from grammar, making vyakaran the essential support (asraya).

notes: The contract captures the two-part structure: (1) words are the sole basis (nibandhanam) of the realities underlying purposive activity, and (2) understanding words requires grammar. The exclusivity particle eva emphasizes the first claim's force.
